text.title: acceptance-criteria Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for acceptance-criteria as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: AC written as: "User can log in." No preconditions, no failure paths, no observable outcome.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Untestable. Every QA engineer will have a different understanding of what this means. GWT forces specificity at every axis.

failure-mode.description: AC containing: "Then the row is inserted into the users table with status='active'."
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Couples the AC to the schema. When the table is renamed or split, the AC breaks even though the behaviour is unchanged. Test at API/UI level: "Then the user can log in successfully."

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Story is too large. When AC count exceeds 7, use the criteria as the slicing signal: split the story at natural boundaries (happy path → story A, error handling → story B).

failure-mode.description: Ambiguous wording survives review: "Fast", "user-friendly", "intuitive" pass through Gherkin syntax but are still untestable.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Linters help but do not catch semantics. Use explicit metrics or measurable outcomes.

failure-mode.description: Scenario explosion: Cartesian-product AC (every role × every state × every error) bloats test-plan.md and slows CI.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening

failure-mode.description: Implementation leakage: "Then the row is inserted into users table" couples AC to schema.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Keep at behaviour level — verify via API, not DB.

failure-mode.description: Drift: AC written once and never updated diverge from production behaviour.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Anchor to living tests; fail CI when AC and test labels mismatch.

failure-mode.description: LLM hallucinated preconditions: Models invent Given conditions even when no such concept exists in the codebase.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always ground in spec/design/code search. Pre-load ERD or schema into the prompt as ground truth.

failure-mode.description: Performance/security NFRs: Easy to write ("response < 200ms") but expensive to verify continuously.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pair with k6/Artillery in CI or mark as periodic gate, or remove the AC entirely.

failure-mode.description: Hallucinated entities: LLMs invent fields not in the model.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Mitigation: pre-load data-analysis.md ERD or design.md schema into the prompt as ground truth.

failure-mode.description: Implementation creep: Agents leak DB/ORM/route names into Gherkin.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Mitigation: post-process with a regex denylist (SELECT|INSERT|UPDATE|/api/|class |def) and reject violators before write.

failure-mode.description: Symmetric coverage bias: Models pad with happy-path duplicates rather than thinking adversarially.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Mitigation: explicit prompt slot "list 3 ways this can fail before writing AC".

failure-mode.description: Test-plan drift: Coding agent updates code → AC stays stale.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always require the same PR to update spec.md AC + test-plan.md test ref + code; pre-commit gate via ac-coverage.sh.

failure-mode.description: Token-cost trap on huge specs: Loading full spec.md every iteration wastes context.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pass only the AC IDs being edited + their direct neighbours.

failure-mode.description: Locale and i18n erasure: Agents default to English/USD examples; AC silently exclude UA/EU contexts.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Add explicit locale matrix per project.

failure-mode.description: Human-in-the-loop checkpoint ignored: Stakeholder sign-off skipped, AC auto-executed.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Do not let automation autonomously generate + execute AC in one chain — split: agent drafts → human reviews → agent executes.

failure-mode.description: Performance NFR theatre: "<200ms" written but never measured.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Either wire to a real benchmark gate (k6, locust, Lighthouse CI) or remove the AC.
